steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures surrounding the blank
steps.content:
Left of blank: chemistry — a noun beginning a noun phrase.

Right of blank: and his colleagues at Temple University recently developed... — a coordinating conjunction plus a second noun phrase, creating a compound subject.

The blank must complete the first element of this compound.

steps.title: Match the rule for title-name pairings in compound subjects
steps.content:
In a compound subject, a title directly followed by a name forms a single noun phrase without intervening punctuation.

Option A correctly places professor Yugang Sun immediately after chemistry with no commas, forming the unified phrase chemistry professor Yugang Sun as the first element of the compound subject paired with his colleagues by and.

Options B, C, and D each violate this rule by inserting commas that either break the title from the name (C, D) or add unnecessary punctuation (B) where none is required.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
